---
title: "Probability"
---

## Why This Module Matters

Probability is the main language of uncertainty, randomness, averages, and concentration. Without it, a large part of modern statistics, ML, control, and learning theory feels like memorized formulas.

::: {.meta-strip}
::: {.meta-box}
**Prerequisites**
Calculus, algebra, and proof comfort
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Unlocks**
Statistics, learning theory, stochastic optimization
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Research Use**
Generalization, uncertainty, random matrices, stochastic models
:::
:::

## Starter Sequence

1. [Random Variables and Distributions](concepts/random-variables-and-distributions.qmd)
2. [Expectation, Variance, Covariance](concepts/expectation-variance-covariance.qmd)
3. Conditional probability and Bayes
4. Limit laws
5. [Concentration and Common Inequalities](concepts/concentration-and-common-inequalities.qmd)

## Big Picture

You do not just want to compute probabilities. You want to think clearly about:

- what is random
- what is conditioned on
- what is being averaged
- how much fluctuation is left
